reviewed_version: v1 (auditor) on coder v3 / codeanalyzer v4 / tester v3
summary: Cross-cutting audit of a 4-stage refactor (singular rename + non-null + accessor reshape + type-entity move). Prior verdicts (codeanalyzer v4 PASS, tester v3 PASS) hold up. Fundamentals are consistent across seams; cache-build safety still proven (via _foldLoaded); test green is real (rebuilt + verified). Three minor/latent codeanalyzer-v4 findings remain in production HEAD because coder v3 was test-only; none blocking. No security review on branch — recommend routing before docs.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — v4 verdict honest; 4 minor notes were rated correctly as latent/cosmetic; cache-build safety re-verified
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — v3 mutation pairs honest (delete-the-throw → exactly-the-named-test-fails); coverage now matches the named invariants
previous_reviews_assessed.security: n/a — not run on this branch; see Finding 1

findings.issue: No security review ran on this branch. Functional security-relevant changes are limited (Permission.Find producer-stamping rehydrated grants; type.Promote throws an InvalidOperationException whose message includes the type name; Wire deserialization mints type from on-wire string). All look low-risk, but convention is to route Permission/Wire-touching branches through security.
findings.impact: Defensive gap. No observed vulnerability. Coverage convention is broken.

findings.issue: type.IsNull => Value == "null" is string-magic. The Null sentinel is a singleton; ReferenceEquals(this, Null) is exact and free. Codeanalyzer v4 F1 flagged this and recommended a one-line fix; coder v3 was test-only, so it's still in HEAD.
findings.impact: Latent footgun if anyone ever constructs new type("null") or a user types `type=null` — silently treated as the sentinel (dropped from wire, cleared on copy).
findings.suggestion: When coder is next invoked, replace with ReferenceEquals(this, Null).

findings.issue: Data.As(string typeName) dropped its `?? AppTypes.GetPrimitiveOrMime(typeName)` fallback, contradicting the ValidateBuild/Sqlite reasoning that kept the same fallback in the entity's own resolver. Codeanalyzer v4 F3.
findings.impact: No production caller today (only the internal error string references it). If one ever appears on a null-context path, it NREs where the old code resolved a primitive.
findings.suggestion: Restore the `?? GetPrimitiveOrMime(typeName)` fallback for consistency, OR document that this overload requires a stamped Data.

findings.issue: Scheme getter changed from `Context?.App?.Type?.Scheme` to `Context.App.Type.Scheme`. Codeanalyzer v4 F4.
findings.impact: Unstamped path-named entity reading .Scheme NREs instead of returning null. All three readers run on stamped entities today, so latent.
findings.suggestion: Either re-add `?.` for graceful null return, or convert to an explicit InvalidOperationException with a producer-bug message (mirror Promote).

findings.issue: Producer-stamping invariant is correct but subtle. Data ctor sets _type directly (bypasses setter, doesn't propagate Context to type). Type's Context is propagated only by the Data.Context setter (line 80-81) when downstream code stamps the Data. Reads on type fold properties (Fields/Values) before Data.Context is stamped will throw.
findings.missed_by: none (codeanalyzer noted producer stamping is correct, didn't trace propagation mechanism explicitly)
findings.impact: Cross-module invariant. Easy to violate in a future module without realizing it. Defense-in-depth is in place via Promote throw + tester's TypeFoldRead_OnUnstampedDomainEntity_ThrowsHard mutation-confirmed test.
findings.suggestion: Capture as a 'Producer Stamping Invariant' note in good_to_know.md so future module authors know. No code change required.
